pages.text: $(o)"Those above the clouds see storms first. They are also alone first."$()$(br2)The $(l)Mountain Weather Observatory$() was the dwelling of Master-class mages — nearest to the sky, farthest from the world.

pages.title: Structure
pages.text: A $(l)Tier 3 Convergence$() circle with 9×9 pillars.$(br2)Perched on mountaintops, usually under perpetual snow. The central core is flanked by four Mana Lantern pillars — even at night, they glow faintly, signaling distant travelers.

pages.title: Loot
pages.text: The center typically hides:$(br)· One $(l)conquest-class$() scroll (Storm-King Writ / Leyline Eruption / Avatar Fall)$(br)· One $(l)Primordial Rune Stone$()$(br)· Often a $(l)Mana Reservoir$() — though usually frozen$(br2)Storm-school mages prize these ruins above all — to summit is to "receive the gift of lightning".

pages.title: What They Truly Watched
pages.text: Observatories did not just track weather — they tracked $(l)leyline-weather synchronization$().$(br2)Records say: when one leyline's current aligned with a specific atmospheric pattern, $(l)twin-pulse resonance$() emerged, briefly boosting circle efficiency.$(br2)The art is lost. But when you activate a summit circle in a thunderstorm, you may feel its echo.
